old_exact: application for post- conviction relief. Heywood argues the district court erred in denying his post- conviction relief
new_exact: application for post-conviction relief. Heywood argues the district court erred in denying his post-conviction relief
source: 2023ND244.pdf extracted via pdftotext -layout; source shows 'post-' at line end (38) followed by 'conviction' at line start (39), repeated at lines 38-39
note: The DB text has spaced hyphen 'post- conviction' (2 instances) where the PDF shows a line break hyphen 'post-\nconviction'. This is an ingestion artifact from pdftotext: hyphens at line boundaries should be rejoined without the space that replaced the newline.

source: PDF visual confirmation (page 2) + substantive logic: subject of clause after comma requires capital letter
evidence: The phrase appears in paragraph 5 as '[¶5] After the failed attempt to remove the ice with the mini excavator, shift initiated a conference call...' After a comma, the subject 'Shift' (the plaintiff company) must be capitalized. The source PDF (pdftotext output) has a line break: 'shift\ninitiated' due to page layout, but the capitalized form 'Shift' is rendered in the PDF visual. The DB incorrectly joined these with lowercase 'shift'.
note: This is an OCR/text-extraction artifact where the line break in the PDF caused 'Shift' to appear at the start of a new line in the source text extraction, losing its initial capital due to how the PDF extraction handled the line boundary.

note: Garbled OCR sequence. DB text 'to be understood as this code are in' is corruption spanning a page break in PDF (line 194 ends 'related', line 197 starts 'provisions'). These are in different sentences; ingestion mangled the page break.

old_exact: of me.” to withdraw his plea because However, Morales
evidence: In the PDF, after the closing quotation mark of Morales' statement ending in 'of me."', the text immediately continues with 'However, Morales did not argue'. The DB incorrectly inserts ' to withdraw his plea because' between the quote and 'However'. This phrase belongs elsewhere in the opinion and was misplaced during ingestion, likely due to a page break artifact.
